message:
fix(share): harden bundle validation, symlink safety, and deploy verification

Comprehensive hardening of the share crate's bundle export, deploy, and
verification pipeline against edge cases discovered during production
use:

Bundle & manifest (lib.rs, bundle.rs):
- Extract load_bundle_manifest_json() to centralize manifest loading
  with symlink-safe path validation via is_real_file/is_real_dir
  (uses symlink_metadata to reject symlinks that could escape the
  bundle directory)
- Refactor load_bundle_export_config() to use the shared loader
- Add test for symlink-to-bundle rejection on Unix

Crypto (crypto.rs):
- Expand cryptographic verification and key handling with additional
  validation paths and error reporting

Deploy (deploy.rs):
- Add validate_unchunked_database_artifacts() for bundles without
  chunking enabled
- Extend validate_chunk_artifacts() to detect unexpected files in the
  chunks/ directory (stale chunks from a previous export, symlinks,
  subdirectories) using symlink_metadata-based inspection
- Build expected chunk path set from manifest and cross-reference
  against actual filesystem entries

Detection & planner (detection.rs, planner.rs):
- Improve database detection heuristics and export plan refinement
- Better handling of edge cases in schema inspection

Prompt (prompt.rs):
- Extend prompt generation with additional context for export workflows

Snapshot (snapshot.rs):
- Add comprehensive test: export_bundle_replaces_stale_owned_outputs
  _but_preserves_unrelated_files — verifies that re-export replaces
  its own artifacts while preserving user-created files in the bundle
  directory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
